text: Recently three-dimensional (3D) organic-inorganic lead halide perovskites have attracted a huge interest in researches for potential use in a wide variety of low-cost and high-efficient optoelectronic devices such as solar cells and lasers. Consequently, several subclasses of halide perovskites have been synthesized like two-dimensional (2D) halide perovskites and have greatly enhanced the knowledge on structure, chemistry, physical and therefore on the properties of halide perovskites and their uses for technology. Materials having a similar chemistry to the 3D halide perovskites exhibit unusual properties due to the two-dimensional structure and the hybrid organic-inorganic interface between the lamellae of the structure leading to a variety of emergent properties. Furthermore, the compatibility of various materials exhibiting diverse dimensionalities offers a mutual beneficial synergetic effect. Many heterostructures and different mixed-dimensional composite materials are consequently highly compatible and their weaknesses can be compensated. In this review we provide an insight in the structure functions relation within 3D and 2D halide perovskites as well as into new functions obtained in 3D-2D mixed dimensional materials obtained using solution-processing method. In conclusion we will discuss possible applications of 3D-2D structures and materials derived thereof in solar cells as well as other applications beyond photovoltaics and give our opinion concerning the benefits offered by mixed dimensional semiconductor perovskite materials in terms of versatility, efficiency and longer lasting lifetime than other conventional electronic materials and devices.
explanation: The text is a high-level, generic review-style passage with occasional awkward phrasing and lacks concrete details or data, which can be indicative of AI-generated text though a human writer could produce similar content.
